description: This figure displays the effect of hydrogen charging on the distributions of cell boundary misorientation ($\theta_{DC}$) and dislocation cell size ($D_{DC}$) for Type 3 dislocation structures in austenitic Fe30Mn6.5Al0.3C low-density steel. Subplot (a) shows the fraction of misorientation (x-axis: 0-2 deg) for uncharged and hydrogen-charged samples: the average $\bar{\theta}_{DC}$ is 0.87° for uncharged samples, and slightly increases to 0.90° for hydrogen-charged samples. Subplot (b) presents the fraction of dislocation cell size (x-axis: 0-500 nm): the average $\bar{D}_{DC}$ is $236\pm10$ nm for uncharged samples, and decreases to $212\pm10$ nm after hydrogen charging. These results indicate that hydrogen induces a slight increase of cell boundary misorientation and obvious refinement of dislocation cell size, supporting that hydrogen alters deformation dislocation structures, consistent with the hydrogen-enhanced microbanding mechanism in the studied steel.
